name: Dzmitry Bahdanau
key_contributions: Invented the attention mechanism for neural machine translation (2014)
Paper "Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate" introduced additive attention
This attention mechanism was the direct precursor to the Transformer's self-attention
Student of Yoshua Bengio at MILA
Demonstrated that neural networks could learn to focus on relevant parts of input sequences
summary: Dzmitry Bahdanau invented the attention mechanism for neural machine translation in 2014, while a student of Yoshua Bengio at MILA. His paper 'Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate' showed that a neural network could learn to selectively attend to relevant parts of an input sequence when generating each word of a translation. This insight — that models should learn where to look — was the direct intellectual ancestor of the self-attention mechanism in the Transformer. Without Bahdanau's attention, 'Attention Is All You Need' would not have been possible.
